div1.entryFree:
Beset, used only as a participle, 1) enclosed so as to prevent escaping: the thicket is b., he cannot scape, Gentl. V, 3, 11.
2) pressed hard, in distress: tell her she is dreadfully b. Lucr. 444. now, daughter Silvia, you are hard b. Gentl. II, 4, 49. O God defend me! how am I b.! Ado IV, 1, 78. I was b. with shame and courtesy, Merch. V, 217. we are b. with thieves, Shr. III, 2, 238. drew to defend him when he was b. Tw. V, 88.
